ucs.title: [LLM] Carbanak (FIN7/Cobalt Group) service-name masquerade — DLL persistence in non-System32 paths
ucs.description: Hunts the Carbanak group's signature persistence: registering a Windows service whose name impersonates legitimate Microsoft services (e.g. 'Sysmain', 'WmiPrvSE', 'WindowsService', 'DefenderUpdate') but whose ImagePath points to a DLL/EXE under AppData, ProgramData, Public, PerfLogs, or %TEMP% — almost always invoked through rundll32.exe. Mandiant/Kaspersky reporting on Carbanak v3 / Anunak and the Carbanak source-code leak repeatedly highlight this exact masquerade-then-rundll32 chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Registry.registry_value_data) as ImagePath values(Registry.process_name) as InstallProc values(Registry.user) as InstallUser from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*" AND Registry.registry_value_name="ImagePath" AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\$Recycle.Bin\\*") AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*rundll32*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*.dll*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*.exe*") by host Registry.registry_path | rex field=Registry.registry_path "Services\\\\(?<ServiceName>[^\\\\]+)" | search ServiceName IN ("Sysmain","SysMain","WmiPrvSE","WindowsService","WindowsManagement","DefenderUpdate","Update","WindowsUpdate","WindowsHelper","NetSvc","NetSvcs","SystemHelper","WinSrv","WinSvc","svchost") OR match(ServiceName,"^[A-Z][a-z]+[A-Z][a-z]+$") | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | table firstTime,lastTime,host,ServiceName,ImagePath,InstallProc,InstallUser
ucs.kql:
// Carbanak / FIN7 service-name masquerade with DLL/EXE in non-System32 path
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\$Recycle.Bin\", @"\Intel\Logs\"]);
let _masqueraded_service_names = dynamic(["Sysmain","SysMain","WmiPrvSE","WindowsService","WindowsManagement","WindowsHelper","WinSrv","WinSvc","NetSvc","NetSvcs","DefenderUpdate","WindowsUpdate","Update","SystemHelper","svchost"]);
let ServiceInstalls = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
    | where RegistryKey has @"\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "ImagePath"
    | where isnotempty(RegistryValueData)
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (_suspect_paths)
    | extend ServiceName = extract(@"(?i)Services\\([^\\]+)(\\ImagePath)?$", 1, RegistryKey)
    | where ServiceName in~ (_masqueraded_service_names)
        or ServiceName matches regex @"^[A-Z][a-z]{1,8}[A-Z][a-z]{1,8}$"   // CamelCase impersonation
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ServiceName, ImagePath = RegistryValueData,
              InstallerProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              InstallerCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InstallerUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
// Optional confirmation: rundll32 spawned from the same suspect path within 24h
let Rundll32Exec = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_suspect_paths)
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.dll['\"]?\s*[,\s]\s*\w+"  // calls an exported function
    | project DeviceName, RunTime = Timestamp, RunCmd = ProcessCommandLine, RunSHA256 = SHA256;
ServiceInstalls
| join kind=leftouter Rundll32Exec on DeviceName
| where isnull(RunTime) or RunTime between (Timestamp - 24h .. Timestamp + 24h)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ServiceName, ImagePath, InstallerProc, InstallerCmd, InstallerUser, RunTime, RunCmd, RunSHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Carbanak / FIN7 service-name masquerade — Sentinel via SecurityEvent 7045 (service install) + 4688 (rundll32)
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let _masqueraded_service_names = dynamic(["Sysmain","SysMain","WmiPrvSE","WindowsService","WindowsManagement","WindowsHelper","WinSrv","WinSvc","NetSvc","NetSvcs","DefenderUpdate","WindowsUpdate","SystemHelper","svchost"]);
let ServiceInstalls = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventID == 7045                                        // service installed
    | extend SvcName  = tostring(parse_xml(EventData).EventData.Data[0]),
             ImagePath = tostring(parse_xml(EventData).EventData.Data[1])
    | where isnotempty(ImagePath)
    | where ImagePath has_any (_suspect_paths)
    | where SvcName in~ (_masqueraded_service_names)
        or SvcName matches regex @"^[A-Z][a-z]{1,8}[A-Z][a-z]{1,8}$"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, SvcName, ImagePath, Account = SubjectUserName;
let Rundll32Exec = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any (_suspect_paths)
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.dll['\"]?\s*[,\s]\s*\w+"
    | project Computer, RunTime = TimeGenerated, RunCmd = CommandLine, RunUser = Account;
ServiceInstalls
| join kind=leftouter Rundll32Exec on Computer
| where isnull(RunTime) or RunTime between (TimeGenerated - 24h .. TimeGenerated + 24h)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SvcName, ImagePath, Account, RunTime, RunCmd
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Carbanak/FIN7 Service Masquerade with DLL in Non-System32 Path
id: 7c4a93ac-ca5b-4b7f-9b8a-5b6e0f7c8d11
status: experimental
description: Detects Windows service installation where the service name impersonates legitimate Microsoft services (Sysmain, WmiPrvSE, WindowsService, etc.) and the ImagePath points to AppData/ProgramData/Public/Temp — Carbanak's signature persistence pattern.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0008/
  - https://securelist.com/the-great-bank-robbery-the-carbanak-apt/68732/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1543.003
  - attack.t1036.004
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0008
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: system
detection:
  selection_event:
    Provider_Name: 'Service Control Manager'
    EventID: 7045
  selection_path:
    ImagePath|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\$Recycle.Bin\'
  selection_name:
    ServiceName:
      - 'Sysmain'
      - 'SysMain'
      - 'WmiPrvSE'
      - 'WindowsService'
      - 'WindowsManagement'
      - 'WindowsHelper'
      - 'DefenderUpdate'
      - 'WindowsUpdate'
      - 'SystemHelper'
      - 'svchost'
      - 'NetSvc'
      - 'NetSvcs'
  condition: selection_event and selection_path and selection_name
falsepositives:
  - Custom enterprise software that installs into ProgramData (validate by SHA256 + signer)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic 'service installed' detections fire dozens of times a day in any enterprise. This rule is bound to the THREE elements that together are nearly unique to Carbanak/Anunak/FIN7's loader: (1) service name impersonates a built-in Windows service, (2) ImagePath lives outside System32 in a writable user-context directory, and (3) the binary on the other end of the ImagePath is invoked via rundll3

ucs.title: [LLM] Carbanak / FIN7 unauthorised remote-admin tool deployment (Ammyy Admin, RMS, TeamViewer) chained from Office or scripting host
ucs.description: Hunts Carbanak's hallmark hands-on-keyboard tooling: the deployment of Ammyy Admin (AA_v3.exe), Remote Manipulator System (rfusclient.exe / rutserv.exe / r_server.exe), or unauthorised TeamViewer/AnyDesk binaries dropped to user-writable paths and launched from an Office or script-host parent. Carbanak/FIN7 has consistently used Ammyy and RMS as their primary RAT layer for fraudulent SWIFT/ATM operations against banks (Kaspersky, Group-IB, Europol — Cobalt Group reports).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("AA_v3.exe","AA_v4.exe","AA_v5.exe","AA_v6.exe","AA_v7.exe","ammyy.exe","ammyy_admin.exe","AmmyyService.exe","rfusclient.exe","rutserv.exe","r_server.exe","RemoteUtilities.exe","AnyDesk.exe","TeamViewer.exe","tv_w32.exe","tv_x64.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","atera.exe") OR Processes.original_file_name IN ("AA.exe","rfusclient.exe","rutserv.exe","TeamViewer.exe","AnyDesk.exe")) by host Processes.process_name Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search (path="*\\AppData\\*" OR path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR path="*\\Temp\\*" OR path="*\\Downloads\\*") OR parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","powershell.exe","hh.exe","regsvr32.exe") | table firstTime,lastTime,host,user,process_name,path,parent_process_name,parent_cmd,cmdline
ucs.kql:
// Carbanak/FIN7 RAT deployment — Ammyy / RMS / TeamViewer / AnyDesk launched from suspect parent or path
let _rat_filenames = dynamic([
    "AA_v3.exe","AA_v4.exe","AA_v5.exe","AA_v6.exe","AA_v7.exe",
    "ammyy.exe","ammyy_admin.exe","AmmyyService.exe",
    "rfusclient.exe","rutserv.exe","r_server.exe",
    "RemoteUtilities.exe","AnyDesk.exe",
    "TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Desktop.exe","tv_w32.exe","tv_x64.exe",
    "ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","AteraAgent.exe"]);
let _rat_products = dynamic(["Ammyy","Remote Manipulator System","Remote Utilities","TeamViewer","AnyDesk","ScreenConnect","Atera"]);
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
let _suspect_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","hh.exe","cmd.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// Identify the RAT either by filename, original name, or product metadata
| where FileName in~ (_rat_filenames)
    or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ (_rat_filenames)
    or ProcessVersionInfoProductName has_any (_rat_products)
// Constrain to deployment context: dropped path OR launched by Office/scripting host
| where FolderPath has_any (_suspect_paths)
    or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_suspect_paths)
    or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_suspect_parents)
// Exclude any sanctioned IT staging path the org has whitelisted
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files\TeamViewer\"   // tune to org-approved deployments
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\TeamViewer\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          RAT          = FileName,
          RatProduct   = ProcessVersionInfoProductName,
          RatPath      = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256  = SHA256,
          ParentImage  = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentPath   = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentUser   = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          IsRemoteSession = AdditionalFields
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Carbanak/FIN7 RAT deployment — Sentinel ASIM (cross-EDR portable)
let _rat_filenames = dynamic(["AA_v3.exe","AA_v4.exe","AA_v5.exe","AA_v6.exe","AA_v7.exe","ammyy.exe","ammyy_admin.exe","AmmyyService.exe","rfusclient.exe","rutserv.exe","r_server.exe","RemoteUtilities.exe","AnyDesk.exe","TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Desktop.exe","tv_w32.exe","tv_x64.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","AteraAgent.exe"]);
let _suspect_paths   = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
let _suspect_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","hh.exe","cmd.exe"]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where TargetProcessName has_any (_rat_filenames)
    or ActingProcessFileOriginalName has_any (_rat_filenames)
    or ActingProcessFileProduct has_any ("Ammyy","Remote Manipulator System","Remote Utilities","TeamViewer","AnyDesk","ScreenConnect","Atera")
| where TargetProcessName has_any (_suspect_paths)
    or ParentProcessName has_any (_suspect_parents)
| where TargetProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files\TeamViewer\"
| where TargetProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\TeamViewer\"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          RAT             = TargetProcessName,
          RatCmd          = TargetProcessCommandLine,
          RatHash         = SHA256,
          ParentImage     = ParentProcessName,
          ParentCmd       = ParentProcessCommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Carbanak/FIN7 Remote Admin Tool Drop & Execute from Office or Scripting Host
id: 9b1e7f30-3a52-4d62-9c7d-2c2b6e7e0f44
status: experimental
description: Detects Ammyy Admin, Remote Manipulator System (RMS), unsanctioned TeamViewer/AnyDesk, or ScreenConnect/Atera launched from a user-writable path or spawned by an Office app or script host — the long-running Carbanak/FIN7 hands-on-keyboard pattern.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0008/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0046/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0080/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.g0008
  - attack.g0046
  - attack.g0080
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_rat:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\AA_v3.exe'
      - '\AA_v4.exe'
      - '\AA_v5.exe'
      - '\AA_v6.exe'
      - '\AA_v7.exe'
      - '\ammyy.exe'
      - '\ammyy_admin.exe'
      - '\AmmyyService.exe'
      - '\rfusclient.exe'
      - '\rutserv.exe'
      - '\r_server.exe'
      - '\RemoteUtilities.exe'
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
      - '\TeamViewer.exe'
      - '\tv_w32.exe'
      - '\tv_x64.exe'
      - '\ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe'
      - '\AteraAgent.exe'
  selection_context:
    - Image|contains:
        - '\AppData\'
        - '\ProgramData\'
        - '\Users\Public\'
        - '\Temp\'
        - '\Downloads\'
    - ParentImage|endswith:
        - '\winword.exe'
        - '\excel.exe'
        - '\powerpnt.exe'
        - '\outlook.exe'
        - '\wscript.exe'
        - '\cscript.exe'
        - '\mshta.exe'
        - '\rundll32.exe'
        - '\regsvr32.exe'
        - '\powershell.exe'
        - '\hh.exe'
  filter_sanctioned:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\TeamViewer\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\TeamViewer\'
  condition: selection_rat and selection_context and not filter_sanctioned
falsepositives:
  - IT helpdesk staging in approved directories — adjust filter_sanctioned per org
level: high
ucs.rationale: Carbanak / Anunak / FIN7 / Cobalt Group share a persistent operational tell: after initial access via spearphishing (CVE-2017-0199, CVE-2017-11882, weaponised RTF/DOC/HTA), they pivot to Ammyy Admin or RMS for hands-on operator control of bank workstations — most documented in Kaspersky's 2015 'Great Bank Robbery' report, Group-IB's Anunak whitepaper, Trustwave's RTM/Cobalt analyses, and the 2018
